title: Chilaquiles Red Salsa

seo.title: How to Make Chilaquiles Red Salsa — Easy Mexican Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Chilaquiles Red Salsa. This easy Mexican recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
longTailKeywords: how to make Chilaquiles Red Salsa
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Mexican Chilaquiles Red Salsa recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Chilaquiles Red Salsa — a beloved Mexican classic

description: Chilaquiles Red Salsa is a vibrant and flavorful Mexican breakfast dish, perfect for starting your day with a kick. Crispy fried tortilla chips are smothered in a rich, spicy red salsa, creating a comforting and satisfying meal. This traditional recipe brings the authentic taste of Mexico right to your kitchen.

ingredients.notes: or 1 can (28 oz) whole peeled tomatoes
adjust to preferred spice level, or 1-2 jalapeños
roughly chopped, plus more for garnish
plus more for frying chips if making homemade

instructions.title: Prepare Tomatoes and Peppers
instructions.text: Wash the Roma tomatoes and serrano peppers. Place them in a medium saucepan and cover with water. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat and cook for about 10-12 minutes, or until the tomatoes are soft and their skins begin to crack. Remove from heat and drain, reserving about 1/4 cup of the cooking liquid.

instructions.title: Blend the Red Salsa
instructions.text: Transfer the cooked tomatoes and serrano peppers to a blender. Add the peeled garlic cloves, roughly chopped white onion, and 1/2 cup of chicken broth (or reserved cooking liquid). Blend until very smooth. If the salsa is too thick, add a little more broth or water until it reaches a pourable consistency.

instructions.title: Cook the Salsa
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large skillet or pot over medium heat. Once hot, carefully pour in the blended salsa. Be cautious as it may splatter. Add 1 teaspoon of salt, or to taste. Bring the salsa to a simmer and cook for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it thickens slightly and the flavors meld. The color should also deepen.

instructions.title: Prepare Tortilla Chips
instructions.text: While the salsa is simmering, if you are making homemade tortilla chips, fry them until golden and crispy. If using store-bought, ensure they are fresh and crunchy. Set aside.

instructions.title: Assemble the Chilaquiles
instructions.text: Just before serving, add the tortilla chips directly into the hot salsa in the skillet. Gently toss the chips with the salsa until they are evenly coated. Cook for 1-2 minutes, just until the chips are softened but still retain some texture. Do not overcook, or they will become soggy.

instructions.title: Serve and Garnish
instructions.text: Divide the chilaquiles among serving plates. Drizzle generously with Crema Mexicana, sprinkle with crumbled queso fresco, and garnish with fresh chopped cilantro and finely diced white onion. For an extra hearty breakfast, top each serving with a freshly fried egg or some shredded chicken.

tips: For a truly authentic flavor, consider charring your tomatoes and serrano peppers on a comal or in a dry skillet before boiling. This adds a smoky depth to the salsa.
Adjust the spice level by adding or removing serrano peppers. For less heat, remove the seeds and veins before boiling; for more, leave them in or add an extra pepper.

To prevent soggy chilaquiles, add the tortilla chips to the hot salsa just before serving, tossing quickly to coat. Alternatively, serve the salsa on the side for individual portions, allowing everyone to add as much as they like.
article:
Awaken your senses to the vibrant flavors of Mexico with a breakfast classic that’s as comforting as it is exhilarating: Chilaquiles. While the dish itself, a delightful medley of fried tortilla chips bathed in salsa, is a star, the true heart and soul lies within its rich, simmering sauce. Today, we’re diving deep into the crimson depths of Chilaquiles Red Salsa, a staple that transforms simple ingredients into an unforgettable culinary experience. Imagine waking up to the aroma of smoky chiles and ripe tomatoes, destined to become the foundation of the best Mexican breakfast you’ve ever had. This isn't just a sauce; it's a celebration of heritage, flavor, and the joy of a truly authentic morning meal. Get ready to master the art of this iconic salsa, and elevate your breakfast game to legendary status.

## What is Chilaquiles Red Salsa?

At its core, Chilaquiles Red Salsa, or salsa roja, is a robust, flavorful sauce made primarily from dried red chiles and ripe tomatoes. Unlike a fresh pico de gallo, this salsa is cooked, allowing its ingredients to meld into a deeply savory and often smoky concoction. The magic begins with selecting the right dried chiles, typically a combination of guajillo and árbol. Guajillos provide a rich, fruity, and slightly smoky base with mild heat, contributing to the salsa's beautiful deep red hue. Árbol chiles, on the other hand, bring the necessary kick, allowing you to customize the spice level to your preference.

This isn't just any red salsa; it's specifically crafted to cling to tortilla chips, infusing them with flavor without making them overly soggy too quickly. Its texture is usually smooth but can be left slightly rustic, depending on preference. While Chilaquiles Red Salsa is famed for its role in the breakfast dish, its versatility extends far beyond. It’s a foundational sauce that embodies the warmth and spice of Mexican cuisine, providing a perfect balance of acidity, sweetness from the tomatoes, and the complex notes of roasted chiles and garlic. It stands in delightful contrast to its tangy green counterpart, salsa verde, offering a different but equally beloved flavor profile that’s essential for any Mexican breakfast enthusiast.

## Ingredients You Need

Crafting the perfect Chilaquiles Red Salsa requires a thoughtful selection of fresh and dried ingredients that build layers of flavor. Here’s what you’ll need to gather:

*   **Dried Guajillo Chiles:** 6-8 large chiles. These are crucial for the salsa's deep color and fundamental fruity, slightly smoky flavor with mild heat.
*   **Dried Chile de Árbol:** 4-6 chiles (adjust to your spice preference). These provide the necessary fiery kick.
*   **Ripe Roma Tomatoes:** 4-5 medium tomatoes. For natural sweetness and body. Roasting them adds depth.
*   **White Onion:** ½ medium, roughly chopped. Aromatic base.
*   **Garlic Cloves:** 3-4 large cloves. Essential for savory depth.
*   **Chicken Broth or Water:** 2-3 cups. For blending and simmering. Chicken broth adds richness.
*   **Vegetable Oil:** 2 tablespoons. For sautéing and frying the salsa.
*   **Salt:** To taste.
*   **Optional Spices:** ½ teaspoon ground cumin, ¼ teaspoon dried oregano (Mexican oregano preferred). These enhance the earthy notes.

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Creating this flavorful Chilaquiles Red Salsa is a process that rewards patience and attention to detail. Follow these steps for a truly authentic taste:

1.  **Prepare the Chiles:** Begin by removing the stems from the guajillo and árbol chiles. For less heat, you can slit them open and remove the seeds, though many prefer to leave the seeds in the árbol chiles for extra spice. Briefly toast the dried chiles in a dry skillet over medium heat for about 30 seconds per side until fragrant. Be careful not to burn them, as this can make the salsa bitter. Immediately transfer the toasted chiles to a bowl and cover them with very hot water. Let them rehydrate for 15-20 minutes until soft and pliable.
2.  **Roast the Tomatoes and Aromatics:** While the chiles are rehydrating, place the Roma tomatoes, ½ onion, and garlic cloves on a baking sheet. You can roast them in an oven at 400°F (200°C) for 20-25 minutes, or char them directly on a comal or cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat until softened and slightly blackened in spots. This roasting step adds a crucial layer of smoky depth to your Chilaquiles Red Salsa.
3.  **Blend the Salsa:** Drain the rehydrated chiles, reserving a little of the soaking liquid. Transfer the softened chiles, roasted tomatoes, onion, and garlic to a blender. Add 2 cups of chicken broth (or water), salt, and any optional spices like cumin and oregano. Blend until completely smooth. If the salsa is too thick, add a little more broth or the reserved chile soaking liquid until it reaches a pourable but still substantial consistency.
4.  **Simmer the Salsa:** Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large pot or deep skillet over medium heat. Carefully pour the blended salsa through a fine-mesh sieve into the hot oil. This step is optional but highly recommended for a smoother salsa, removing any remaining bits of chile skin or seeds. Stir constantly for a minute or two to prevent sticking.
5.  **Cook to Perfection:** Bring the salsa to a gentle simmer. Reduce the heat to low, cover partially, and let it cook for at least 20-30 minutes, stirring occasionally. This simmering time is vital; it allows the flavors to meld, deepen, and concentrate, transforming the raw blended ingredients into a rich, complex Chilaquiles Red Salsa. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed, adding more salt if necessary. The salsa should thicken slightly as it simmers.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving truly exceptional Chilaquiles Red Salsa goes beyond just following a recipe. Here are some pro tips to elevate your creation:

*   **Quality Chiles Matter:** Invest in fresh, pliable dried chiles. Old, brittle chiles will yield a duller flavor. Toasting them briefly unlocks their aromatic oils, but be very careful not to burn them, as this will impart a bitter taste.
*   **Roast for Depth:** Don't skip roasting the tomatoes, onion, and garlic. Charring them slightly adds a smoky, caramelized depth that cannot be achieved by simply boiling. This step is a game-changer for the richness of your Chilaquiles Red Salsa.
*   **Sieve for Smoothness:** While optional, passing the blended salsa through a fine-mesh sieve before simmering results in a silky-smooth sauce. This removes any tough bits of chile skin or seeds, contributing to a more refined texture.
*   **Simmer Time is Key:** The longer you simmer the salsa (within reason), the better the flavors will develop and deepen. A quick simmer won't allow the ingredients to fully meld. Aim for at least 20-30 minutes, and even longer if you have the time, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking.
*   **Use Good Broth:** Opt for a good quality chicken broth over plain water for blending and simmering. The broth adds an extra layer of savory richness that complements the chiles and tomatoes beautifully.
*   **Make Ahead:** Chilaquiles Red Salsa actually tastes even better the next day, as the flavors continue to marry. Prepare a larger batch and store it in the refrigerator for up to a week, or freeze it for longer storage, making future breakfast preparations a breeze.

## Serving Suggestions

Once you've perfected your Chilaquiles Red Salsa, the most classic way to enjoy it is, of course, with chilaquiles! Fry or bake corn tortilla chips until crispy, then toss them generously in the warm salsa just before serving. Pile them high on a plate and garnish with traditional toppings: a generous crumble of queso fresco or cotija cheese, a drizzle of Mexican crema (or sour cream thinned with a little milk), thinly sliced red onion, and fresh cilantro. A perfectly fried egg, with its runny yolk, is the ultimate crowning glory, adding richness and protein to this indulgent breakfast.

Beyond chilaquiles, this versatile red salsa can transform many dishes. Serve it as a vibrant sauce for huevos rancheros, smother over breakfast burritos, or use it as a base for chicken or cheese enchiladas. It also makes an excellent topping for grilled chicken or pork, adding a touch of authentic Mexican flavor to your dinner plate.

## Variations and Substitutions

The beauty of Chilaquiles Red Salsa lies in its adaptability. Don't be afraid to experiment and make it your own:

*   **Chile Choices:** While guajillo and árbol are classic, you can introduce other dried chiles for different flavor profiles. Add a rehydrated ancho chile for a deeper, smokier, and fruitier note, or a pasilla chile for a rich, earthy flavor. If you prefer less heat, reduce the number of chile de árbol or omit them entirely. For more heat, add a fresh serrano or jalapeño during the roasting step.
*   **Tomato Alternatives:** If fresh Roma tomatoes aren't available or you're short on time, a 15-ounce can of fire-roasted diced tomatoes can be a good substitute, adding a similar smoky depth.
*   **Spice It Up (or Down):** Adjust the amount of chile de árbol to control the heat. For a milder salsa, remove all seeds from the chiles. For extra spice, leave them in and even add a pinch of cayenne pepper.
*   **Herbaceous Notes:** A sprig of epazote, a traditional Mexican herb, can be added to the salsa during simmering for an authentic, slightly anise-like flavor.
*   **Vegetarian/Vegan:** Easily make this salsa vegetarian or vegan by using vegetable broth instead of chicken broth. The flavor will still be incredibly rich and satisfying.
*   **Texture Control:** For a chunkier salsa, blend for a shorter time. For a super smooth salsa, ensure all ingredients are well-blended and pass through a fine-mesh sieve.

Mastering Chilaquiles Red Salsa is a delicious journey into the heart of Mexican cuisine. It's more than just a recipe; it's an invitation to explore vibrant flavors, embrace traditional techniques, and create memorable meals. Whether you're a seasoned chef or a home cook looking to expand your repertoire, this robust and satisfying salsa is a culinary triumph waiting to happen. So gather your ingredients, fire up your stove, and prepare to delight your taste buds with the authentic taste of Mexico. Your breakfast table will never be the same!
